kategorie.label: Kategorie
kategorie.description:
One selected option of multiple available options (or `null`/`None`).

The available options are defined in the `lookup_data` attribute of this control.

The field value will be the key in this `lookup_data` attribute (or `null`/`None`).

Displayed as single-select field in HTML pages.

One selected option of multiple available options (or `null`/`None`).

partner.label: Partner
partner.description:
A reference to a LivingApp record from the target lookup app 'Partner' with the id `6a463107a68c98dae897d235` (or `null`/`None`).

The field value will be a valid record URL in the form `https://my.living-apps.de/rest/apps/6a463107a68c98dae897d235/records/<record-id>` (or `null`/`None`).

Displayed as single-select field in HTML pages.

ansprechpartner.label: Ansprechpartner
ansprechpartner.description:
A reference to a LivingApp record from the target lookup app 'Ansprechpartner' with the id `6a46310a1a639283f3347b68` (or `null`/`None`).

The field value will be a valid record URL in the form `https://my.living-apps.de/rest/apps/6a46310a1a639283f3347b68/records/<record-id>` (or `null`/`None`).

Displayed as single-select field in HTML pages.

startdatum.label: Startdatum
startdatum.description:
A calendar date consisting of year, month and day (or `null`/`None`)

The field value will be a string in the format `<YYYY>-<MM>-<DD>` (or `null`/`None`).

For example: `2000-02-29`.

For `POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH` requests the REST API uses a liberal parsing approach so that strings like `2026`, `February 29, 2000 12:34 MEST`, `letztes Jahr` etc. will be accepted as value date values. However this runs the risk of parsing ambiguous values incorrectly. To ensure reliable parsing it is best to use the canonical format specified above.

enddatum.label: Enddatum
enddatum.description:
A calendar date consisting of year, month and day (or `null`/`None`)

The field value will be a string in the format `<YYYY>-<MM>-<DD>` (or `null`/`None`).

For example: `2000-02-29`.

For `POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH` requests the REST API uses a liberal parsing approach so that strings like `2026`, `February 29, 2000 12:34 MEST`, `letztes Jahr` etc. will be accepted as value date values. However this runs the risk of parsing ambiguous values incorrectly. To ensure reliable parsing it is best to use the canonical format specified above.

vertragsdokument.label: Vertragsdokument
vertragsdokument.description:
An uploaded file (or `null`/`None`).

The field value will be the URL to download the file in the form `https://my.living-apps.de/files/6a46310befb6490de8bc48c2/data/<record-id>/<control-identifier>/<internal-id>` (or `null`/`None`).
